expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report identifies or classifies GOLD/Barrick as a commodity producer or miner
expectations.evidence: Title: 'GOLD (Barrick Mining, NYSE: B)'. Dedicated commodity analysis section treats it as commodity producer throughout.

expectations.text: Report discusses gold commodity price before or prominently alongside company analysis
expectations.evidence: Line 7: '## Gold Commodity Price Analysis (First)' — a dedicated section BEFORE the Recommendation, with spot gold ~$5,172/oz and full AISC margin calculation

expectations.text: Report mentions AISC (all-in sustaining cost) or production cost/breakeven
expectations.evidence: Line 10: 'Barrick FY2025 gold AISC = $1,637/oz; Q4 gold AISC = $1,581/oz; 2026 guide $1,760-$1,950/oz'

expectations.text: Report explains miner leverage to underlying commodity (how gold moves amplify in miners)
expectations.evidence: Line 14: 'a 5% move in gold (~$259/oz) changes unit margin by ~7.6%-8.0% vs current implied margin base, then operating leverage and fixed-cost absorption amplify equity impact'

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section
expectations.evidence: Lines 56-66: Valuation Summary with P/E 12.16x, EV/EBITDA 8.57x, P/S 4.53x, plus historical averages and peer medians

expectations.text: Report discusses gold price trend or outlook as part of the thesis
expectations.evidence: Line 15: 'this is still mostly a gold call. If gold holds above ~$4,500 (Barrick planning assumption), Barrick remains structurally cash generative'

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
expectations.evidence: Lines 161-178: 16 sources with hyperlinks including Barrick IR, StockAnalysis, TradingEconomics, AlphaQuery

expectations.text: Recommendation rationale connects back to the gold commodity thesis
expectations.evidence: Line 27: 'Gold pricing remains far above Barrick's AISC/breakeven framework, supporting strong FCF and shareholder returns'

claims.evidence: Consistent with TradingEconomics source and matches old_skill's ~$5,186 (small difference likely timing)
Consistent across both outputs and sourced from Barrick IR press release

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the report calculates an explicit per-ounce margin (spot - AISC). The with_skill version does this ($3,535/oz) and it's arguably the most important number for a commodity producer. Consider adding an assertion for explicit margin calculation.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The commodity-specific assertions (AISC, miner leverage, gold-first analysis) are genuinely discriminating — the with_skill version produced a dedicated gold section with quantitative margin and leverage calculations, which is precisely what the improved skill was designed to elicit.
